content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. You must perform some (possibly zero) operations to make the elements of AAA distinct.

In one operation, you can either:


Remove one element from the beginning of the array AAA and append any positive integer to the end.
Or remove one element from the end of the array AAA and prepend any positive integer to the beginning.
Find the minimum number of operations required to make all the elements of the array distinct.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations required to make all the elements distinct.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:
Initially A=[1,3,3,5,1,2,4,1]A = [1, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, 1]A=[1,3,3,5,1,2,4,1]. Consider the following sequence of operations.


[1,3,3,5,1,2,4,1‾]→prepend 6remove 1[6,1,3,3,5,1,2,4][1, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, \underline{1}] \xrightarrow[\mathrm{prepend} \, 6]{\mathrm{remove} \, 1} [\boldsymbol 6, 1, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4][1,3,3,5,1,2,4,1​]remove1prepend6​[6,1,3,3,5,1,2,4]


[6‾,1,3,3,5,1,2,4]→append 7remove 6[1,3,3,5,1,2,4,7][\underline{6}, 1, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4] \xrightarrow[\mathrm{append} \, 7]{\mathrm{remove} \, 6} [1, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, \boldsymbol 7][6​,1,3,3,5,1,2,4]remove6append7​[1,3,3,5,1,2,4,7]


[1‾,3,3,5,1,2,4,7]→append 8remove 1[3,3,5,1,2,4,7,8][\underline{1}, 3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, 7] \xrightarrow[\mathrm{append} \, 8]{\mathrm{remove} \, 1} [3, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, 7, \boldsymbol 8][1​,3,3,5,1,2,4,7]remove1append8​[3,3,5,1,2,4,7,8]


[3‾,3,5,1,2,4,7,8]→append 9remove 3[3,5,1,2,4,7,8,9][\underline{3}, 3, 5, 1, 2, 4, 7, 8] \xrightarrow[\mathrm{append} \, 9]{\mathrm{remove} \, 3} [3, 5, 1, 2, 4, 7, 8, \boldsymbol 9][3​,3,5,1,2,4,7,8]remove3append9​[3,5,1,2,4,7,8,9]


All the elements of AAA are now distinct. It can be shown that it is not possible in less than 444 operations.
Test case 222: No operations are required since all the elements of AAA are already distinct.
